article_meta.title: Shopping These Giant Yankee Candles are 40% off before Valentine's Day By Miska Salemann Published Feb

reasoning: The summary remains neutral and avoids the article’s promotional, emotive language, using measured phrasing and occasional attribution (e.g., “positioned as”) instead of adopting the salesy framing.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs like “are available,” “offering,” and attributes praise rather than asserting it.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Claims (discount, burn time, reviews) reflect article content; affiliate disclosure noted.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Subjective framing (“perfect gift”) is hedged as the article’s positioning, not the model’s assertion.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Avoids the article’s emotive language (“euphoria,” “swoon”); tone stays informational.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal or ad hominem remarks.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: No competing perspectives to privilege; mentions affiliate disclosure and other deals factually.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Minor omission in sources_represented (does not list customer reviews separately).
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Minimal; “perfect” appears but is clearly attributed (“positioned as”).
custom_reasoning.word_choice: No politically loaded or ideologically charged terms.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced; mirrors the article’s positive, deal-focused content without exaggeration.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No deference or dismissal of any source category.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than the promotional source, with attribution where needed.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures main angles (deal details, reviews, affiliate note) but sources_represented lists only the outlet.
